figures.caption: Fig. 5: Composite image of the NGC6781 planetary nebula, captured with a Stellina and a Vespera: 1590 frames of 10 seconds captured during several nights (13/8/2022, 25/8/2023, 30/8/2023, 6/10/2023, 1/7/2024, 25/8/2025).
Fig. 1: Distribution of image dimensions (width, height) in the test2026 split.

Fig. 2: Composite image of M1 supernova remnant, captured with a Stellina and a Vespera: 1752 frames of 10 seconds captured during several nights (1/3/2022, 2/3/2022, 16/12/2022, 8/2/2023, 3/4/2023, 5/11/2023, 17/12/2023, 19/3/2024, 27/12/2024, 28/12/2024, 3/2/2025).
Fig. 4: Composite image of the NGC4490 spiral galaxy, captured with a Stellina and a Vespera: 1590 frames of 10 seconds captured during several nights (9/6/2023, 23/6/2023, 18/3/2024, 3/4/2025).

Fig. 3: Composite image of M4 globular cluster, captured with a Stellina and a Vespera: 776 frames of 10 seconds captured during several nights (4/7/2023, 30/6/2024, 20/8/2024, 28/6/2025, 29/6/2025, 18/8/2025).
